description: A shape (record/struct-like type) with one or more typed fields and no behavior. Validated against the real transpiler across 20 variations (different shape names and 2-4 fields each, mixed primitive/pointer/text field types), all parse/emit/compile clean.

fields.desc: list of [field_name, dictum_type] pairs, e.g. [["A", "whole number"], ["B", "raw pointer to whole number"]] -- rendered as one indented `field_name as dictum_type` line per entry

requires: Every field name must be unique within the shape.
common_mistakes: Do not invent additional fields the plan didn't ask for.

Do not reference another shape's fields from outside an action on that shape via bracket/dot interpolation like `[ShapeName.field]` inside a string -- Dictum has no such interpolation syntax (see the shape-actions pattern's notes for the concrete failure this refers to).
example:
shape Calculator holds
    A as whole number
    B as whole number
    Sum as whole number
end shape
